repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md §2.2 HomogeneousCollapse: generic homogeneous-response collapse lemma

published_prior_art.cite: SunAbraham2021 / CallawaySantAnna2021 / Wooldridge2021 homogeneous or saturated agreement regimes

repo_prior_art.cite: closest catalogue entries panel_honest_did_phase and q1_spectral_phase_transition are row-space diagnostics for different objects, not four-estimator IF equivalence

published_prior_art.cite: BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024 gives efficient imputation and restricted equivalences; ChenSantAnnaXie2025 gives EIFs; RothSantAnna2023Efficient gives efficient rollout adjustment; none states the four-estimator iff row-space frontier
BorusyakJaravelSpiess2024 and RothSantAnna2023Efficient show efficiency gains/dominance, but not the proposed common-model projection-quadratic gap formula for BJS/SA/CS/W

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The common model M_PT(a) allows unrestricted covariance/tangent perturbations, but the BJS efficiency result being mapped to V_EIF is only established in the cited literature under spherical or known-GLS-style linear-efficiency benchmarks; pin the covariance/tangent restriction or weaken the BJS-attains-bound clause.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Narrow M_PT(a) to the exact efficiency benchmark used for the BJS bound, or restate Conjecture 2 as a comparison to the BJS linear-unbiased benchmark rather than a full semiparametric EIF bound.
Add a worked three-period, two-cohort example giving explicit Z_M and P_M for at least SA and BJS, so the projection-loss term is visibly computable rather than only existentially named.

Add one sentence comparing the proposed variance-gap formula to BJS Section 5.3 efficiency comparisons and Roth-Sant'Anna's weak-dominance result, emphasizing that the new object is an iff active-row-space formula.

recommended_next_step: Revise Conjecture 2's efficiency benchmark/model restriction, then rerun novelty review.
